channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: You're going to see more 'city-killer' asteroids, but fret not: MIT astronomer
item.description: This week's hype around a "city-killing" asteroid possibly striking Earth raised the fears of many before the odds of impact dropped to nearly nothing, but the MIT scientist who developed the risk scale for this type of event said it still has a lot to teach the public.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WGN 9

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Woman accused of drugging and robbing older men in a deadly romance scheme
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A woman used online dating apps to lure at least four older men to meet her in person, then drugged them with sedatives and stole hundreds of thousands of dollars in a “sinister” romance scheme, FBI officials in Las Vegas said Friday.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL, JESSE BEDAYN and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge upholds ban on DOGE accessing sensitive Treasury information, for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge in Manhattan on Friday extended a ban on Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency accessing sensitive Treasury Department information, but rejected broader restrictions sought by 19 Democratic state attorneys general who sued over the issue. Judge Jeannette A.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: National Park Service restores some jobs of fired employees, pledges to hire 7,700 seasonal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is restoring jobs for dozens of National Park Service employees fired amid government-wide reductions and hiring nearly 3,000 additional seasonal workers, following an uproar over an aggressive plan to downsize the agency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The justices said in an unsigned order that Hampton Dellinger, head of the Office of Special Counsel, could remain in his job at least until Feb. 26. That’s when a lower-court order temporarily protecting him expires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The Latest: Trump signals he’s open to multiple budget bills instead of just one
item.description: Hours after the Senate approved a spending framework, President Donald Trump said in a Fox News interview that he’s open to approving the spending plan in multiple bills “as long as we get to the same point.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles DA gives update in Menendez brothers' resentencing case
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney said Friday he hasn't yet made a decision on whether Lyle and Erik Menendez should be resentenced in their 1996 conviction for the murder of their wealthy parents at their Beverly Hills home.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: Six weeks after devastating wildfires, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the city’s fire chief amid a public rift over preparations for the fires and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Michael Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: US housing market worth nearly $50 trillion, over double a decade ago
item.description: The total value of all U.S. homes has doubled over the past decade. These cities saw the fastest growth last year.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens to withhold federal funds from Maine over transgender athletes
item.description: In a statement, Maine Gov. Janet Mills, a Democrat, said the state “will not be intimidated” by Trump’s threat to withhold its federal funds.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: LA district attorney opposes new trial for Menendez brothers, still deciding on resentencing
item.description: The Los Angeles district attorney says he not yet decided whether to recommend resentencing Lyle and Erik Menendez.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court clears the way for a lawsuit over COVID-19 pandemic-era unemployment claims in Alabama
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — People left waiting for months on their unemployment claims during the coronavirus pandemic in Alabama must be able to sue the state over the delay, the U.S. Supreme Court said Friday. The 5-4 ruling comes after the Alabama Supreme Court rejected a lawsuit from 21 people.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maui resort explosion at grill area injures 7
item.description: An explosion rocked a grill area at The Whaler resort in Maui, Hawaii, leaving multiple people injured and debris scattered across Kaanapali Beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How LA wildfires are making an already tough rental market even worse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The one-bedroom cottage with a woodsy vibe reminded Heather McAlpine of the home she lost to the brutal Los Angeles-area wildfires. But only two hours after seeing the listing, the rental was snapped up.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and DAMIAN DOVARGANES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers 'grateful' Netflix spotlighted family abuse
item.description: Lyle Menendez said Netflix's series, “Monsters: The Lyle and Erik Menendez Story,” "opened a lot of people's eyes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie declined to testify in his defense Thursday as his lawyers rested their case without calling any witnesses.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at $4B in funding for California high-speed rail
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Trump administration is once again targeting California's controversial high-speed rail project, with federal transportation officials on Thursday announcing an investigation and possible withdrawal of about $4 billion in federal funding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lifetime, Chili’s celebrate National Margarita Day with new rom-com feature
item.description: Love, laughter, and lime wedges take center stage in Lifetime’s latest feel-good romance, created in partnership with Chili’s Bar & Grill, to celebrate National Margarita Day, which is coming up on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A flight to a Toronto paramedics conference becomes a real emergency for a former EMT
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former paramedic Peter Carlson is still having difficulty putting into perspective the shuddering jolt he and 79 others aboard Delta Air Lines flight 4819 experienced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: 23% of Americans ended a relationship over money: Survey
item.description: A new report shows nearly 1 in 4 Americans have ended a relationship because they weren't on the same page when it comes to finances.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Snowy roads lead to hundreds of Virginia and North Carolina crashes as Arctic air brings record cold
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Roads in Virginia and North Carolina remained treacherous Thursday as unusually heavy snow led to hundreds of accidents, including crashes on an interstate that involved more than 50 vehicles.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, MAKIYA SEMINERA and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 people are dead in a small plane collision at a southern Arizona airport
item.description: A midair collision involving two small planes in southern Arizona killed two people Wednesday morning, authorities said. Federal air-safety investigators said each plane had two people aboard when they collided at Marana Regional Airport on the outskirts of Tucson.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navy veteran's service dogs denied access on plane in Denver
item.description: A Navy veteran missed his flight after not being allowed to take his service dogs on a plane from Denver to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Shaul Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Hanceville police chief, 4 officers and spouse arrested in investigation following dispatcher's overdose death
item.description: Autopsy results show a Hanceville dispatcher found dead in his office in August 2024 died from the combined toxic effects of multiple drugs.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: A new article published this week in The Lancet medical journal found that more than half of the world's population lacks access to safe and affordable medical oxygen services, and most people outside of high-income countries go without adequate oxygen services for their medical conditions.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order asking for recommendations on lowering IVF costs
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order directing federal agencies to find ways to reduce the high cost of in vitro fertilization (IVF) treatment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dozens of police recruits nationwide have died while training in last decade: probe
item.description: At least 29 recruits died during basic training at law enforcement academies around the country in the last decade, an AP investigation found.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9
